procedures.description: Established Patient Office or Other Outpatient Visit With High Level of Medical Decision Making, if Using Time, 40 Minutes or More

summary: This document itemizes professional charges for patient Jane Doe at Mayo Clinic, with a statement date of January 15, 2025. Services provided on July 3, 2024, included an established patient office visit and the administration of Ciprofloxacin IV, totaling $612.00 in charges. After payments and adjustments, a remaining balance of $538.56 is due for these services.

medical_terms.meaning: A Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) code used to bill for an established patient office or outpatient visit involving a high level of medical decision-making or at least 40 minutes of total time.
A Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) code for Ciprofloxacin, injection, 200 mg. It is often used in billing for medications administered.

Ciprofloxacin is a broad-spectrum antibiotic medication administered intravenously (IV) to treat a variety of severe bacterial infections, including respiratory tract infections, urinary tract infections, and skin infections.

patient_advice: Review the itemized services carefully and compare them with any explanation of benefits (EOB) from your insurance provider.
Contact Mayo Clinic at 1-844-217-9591 if you have any questions about the charges or your remaining balance.
recommended_questions: Is this outstanding balance my full responsibility, or will my insurance cover a portion of it?
Can you provide a more detailed breakdown of the Ciprofloxacin IV charge, including the exact dosage and unit cost?
